record.field: Twenty is a modern, fully open-source CRM and a credible alternative to Salesforce and HubSpot, built around a flexible spreadsheet-like data model.

Twenty is a modern, fully open-source CRM and a credible alternative to Salesforce and HubSpot, built around a flexible spreadsheet-like data model.

Twenty gives teams the core building blocks of a CRM — customizable objects, records, views, pipelines and workflows — on top of a PostgreSQL-native data model, so you can define unlimited custom objects and relationships without consultants or vendor lock-in. It targets startups and technical teams that want full ownership of their customer data and the ability to extend the platform as code. Its modern React interface with a NestJS/GraphQL backend, runtime data-model customization and built-in workflow automation set it apart from heavier legacy CRMs.

Key features
  • Flexible, PostgreSQL-native data model with unlimited custom objects and relationships
  • Spreadsheet-style records with kanban, table and timeline views
  • No-code workflow automation, triggers and webhooks
  • AI agents and chat capabilities integrated into the CRM
  • Email and calendar sync via your own OAuth credentials (Gmail, Outlook)
  • Self-hosted via Docker Compose with a permissive AGPL-3.0 license and no user caps
Best for

Startups, sales teams and technical organizations that want a self-hosted, customizable CRM with full data ownership and the freedom to extend it without per-seat licensing or vendor lock-in.

services: twenty-change-vol-ownership: image: ubuntu user: root volumes: - ./twenty_server_local_data:/tmp/server-local-data - ./twenty_docker_data:/tmp/docker-data command: 'bash -c " chown -R 1000:1000 /tmp/server-local-data && chown -R 1000:1000 /tmp/docker-data"
' container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_twenty_change_vol_ownership' restart: unless-stopped networks: - internal twenty-server: image: twentycrm/twenty:latest volumes: - ./twenty_server_local_data:/app/packages/twenty-server/${STORAGE_LOCAL_PATH - ./twenty_docker_data:/app/docker-data environment: PORT: 3000 SERVER_URL: {{ http_protocol }}://{{ twenty_host }} FRONT_BASE_URL: {{ http_protocol }}://{{ twenty_host }} PG_DATABASE_URL: postgres://{{ db_user }}:{{ db_password }}@twenty-postgres:5432/twenty ENABLE_DB_MIGRATIONS: 'true' REDIS_URL: redis://twenty-redis:6379 IS_SIGN_UP_DISABLED: ${IS_SIGN_UP_DISABLED:-false} SIGN_IN_PREFILLED: 'false' IS_CONFIG_VARIABLES_IN_DB_ENABLED: 'true' STORAGE_TYPE: local APP_SECRET: {{ app_secret }} depends_on: twenty-change-vol-ownership: condition: service_completed_successfully twenty-postgres: condition: service_healthy healthcheck: test: - CMD - curl - -f - http://127.0.0.1:3000/healthz interval: 5s timeout: 5s retries: 30 start_period: 30s restart: always container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:3000' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network twenty-worker: image: twentycrm/twenty:latest command: - yarn - worker:prod environment: PG_DATABASE_URL: postgres://{{ db_user }}:{{ db_password }}@twenty-postgres:5432/twenty SERVER_URL: {{ http_protocol }}://{{ twenty_host }} FRONT_BASE_URL: {{ http_protocol }}://{{ twenty_host }} REDIS_URL: redis://twenty-redis:6379 ENABLE_DB_MIGRATIONS: 'false' STORAGE_TYPE: local APP_SECRET: {{ app_secret }} depends_on: twenty-postgres: condition: service_healthy twenty-server: condition: service_healthy restart: always container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_twenty_worker' networks: - internal twenty-postgres: image: postgres:17-alpine volumes: - ./twenty_postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data environment: POSTGRES_USER: {{ db_user }} POSTGRES_PASSWORD: {{ db_password }} POSTGRES_DB: twenty healthcheck: test: - CMD-SHELL - pg_isready -U {{ db_user }} -d twenty interval: 5s timeout: 5s retries: 10 restart: always container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_twenty_postgres' networks: - internal twenty-redis: image: redis:latest volumes: - ./twenty_redis_data:/data healthcheck: test: - CMD - redis-cli - ping interval: 5s timeout: 5s retries: 10 restart: always container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_twenty_redis' networks: - internal networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump consistente de PostgreSQL (servicio en marcha) → $BACKUP_DATA_DIR docker compose exec -T twenty-postgres sh -c 'pg_dump -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --no-owner -Fc' \ > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump" echo "pg_dump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Levanta solo la DB, espera readiness y restaura docker compose up -d twenty-postgres for i in $(seq 1 30); do docker compose exec -T twenty-postgres sh -c 'pg_isready -U "$POSTGRES_USER"' && break sleep 2 done docker compose exec -T twenty-postgres sh -c 'pg_restore -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --clean --if-exists --no-owner' \ < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.dump" # Verificación: la DB no quedó vacía (sh -c con dobles comillas; $VAR del contenedor con \$) N=$(docker compose exec -T twenty-postgres sh -c "psql -U \$POSTGRES_USER -d \$POSTGRES_DB -tAc \"SELECT count(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema='public'\"") echo "tablas restauradas: $N" test "$N" -gt 0
